review: We cut our standup from 15 minutes to 0. The Slack check-in takes ~60 seconds and the summary reads like a human wrote it.
Finally, a standup bot that respects time zones. People reply when they start their day and I get a clean digest.
The summaries are scary good and the 'blocker' pings helped me clear two issues before lunch.
I was skeptical of another bot, but StandupSpark feels polite and low-noise.
Setup with Jira took me a minute to figure out the right permissions.
Our team stopped doing the daily Zoom standup. No one misses it.
The nudge is gentle, not naggy, and skips PTO automatically.
We work across SF, Berlin, and SG; the timezone scheduling just works.
At first the channel felt busy; the one-click 'summary only' setting fixed it.
I love the 'catch up' brief when I get back from meetings.
Being private by default matters—people answer in DM and share more.
Helps me spot blockers fast without chasing people.
Could use more templates for non-engineering teams.
The analytics on participation are simple and useful.
We switched from Geekbot; fewer pings and better summaries.
The bot skipped me on a holiday without me doing anything.
Took a couple of tries to tweak questions for our design team.
Wish there was SSO; they told me it's on the Business plan.
Feels human. The tone is friendly, not corporate.
Connecting GitHub surfaced PRs in the summary, which I love.
